# AGENTS.md — figure-agent

Codex entry point. Authoritative workflow: `skills/figure-agent/SKILL.md`.
Before product work, read the sole product and execution authority
`docs/figure-agent.md`. Other specifications, plans, roadmaps, and milestones
are evidence, not product authority.

## Identity

`figure-agent` is a **paper-figure quality kernel**. A human, Codex, GPT, Claude, image-generation tool, Illustrator, or any other workflow may author the TikZ source; the plugin's durable job is to enforce style and reproducibility invariants regardless of author.

Quality kernel responsibilities:

1. **Style Lock** — palette / font / stroke / macro-usage consistency across a manuscript's figures.
2. **Compile & Export reliability** — deterministic PDF / SVG / TIFF / PNG output with stale-artifact detection.
3. **Visual QA** — collision and visual-clash checks; spec-driven golden-fixture artifact gates.
4. **Reproducibility** — per-figure folder contract, transparent state inference (`/fig_status`), explicit export tracking policy for golden fixtures.

Plugin does **not**:
- Call image-gen or external vision APIs directly.
- Decide which frontier LLM/agent to use.
- Convert PNG → SVG/TikZ automatically (see `docs/figure-agent.md`).

Plugin **does** delegate vision tasks to the host main loop (L4.5 vision
critique). The host reads the build PNG and writes `critique.md` using
subscription tokens — no external API call originates from plugin code.

## Workflow

**Active (quality kernel + L4.5 vision critique):**
```
/fig_new <name>       scaffold (briefing + spec)
/fig_extract <name>   optional Layer 2.5 — OCR + palette shape clusters from reference PNG
                      → coordinate_hints.yaml (recommended when spec.yaml.reference_image exists)
                      [user/LLM authors examples/<name>/<name>.tex]
/fig_compile <name>   lint + lualatex + collision/clash checks
                      + perception data pack in build/perception/
                      (FIGURE_AGENT_STRICT=1 promotes findings to hard fail)
/fig_critique <name>  L4.5 — host reads build/<name>.png + briefing,
                      writes structured critique.md (report-only)
/fig_export <name>    PDF / SVG / TIFF / PNG export (dvisvgm preserves text)
/fig_status [<name>]  read-only stage + accepted-state inference
```

The v0.1 frozen helpers (`/fig_prompt`, `/fig_preview_select`,
prompt-template / redaction / selection-notes pipeline,
`spec.yaml.selected_preview` stage gate) were removed in the v0.2
cleanup (PR #8a + #8b). The v0.1 `/fig_review` HALT-then-paste workflow
was renamed and extended into `/fig_critique` (PR #9). See
`docs/architecture-v0.2-proposal.md`.

## Repo Notes

- ResearchOS workspace sibling to `[Athena]/`, `[Graph_making_hub]/`.
- Successor to `[tikz-paper-workflow]/` (archived 2026-04-27).
- Reference-layer architecture deprecated (Y0 pilot evidence).
- v0.1 spec: `docs/historical/design-v0.1.md` (frozen historical).
- Product and forward execution authority: `docs/figure-agent.md`.
- Operational reference:
  `docs/architecture-v0.5-per-panel-reference-workflow.md`.

%% 황 폴리머 논문 figure 표준 프리앰블 + Paul Tol Muted 팔레트
%% Source: sc7_concept_tikz_v7.tex (paper-grade benchmark, 2026-04-24)
%% Engine: lualatex (primary) — xelatex은 fontspec+Arial 렌더링 불일치로 미사용
%% Usage:  \usepackage{polymer-paper-preamble}  % in preamble

\NeedsTeXFormat{LaTeX2e}
\ProvidesPackage{polymer-paper-preamble}[2026/04/24 Polymer paper figure preamble]

%% --- Fonts (requires lualatex or xelatex) ---
\usepackage{fontspec}
\setmainfont{Arial}
\setsansfont{Arial}

%% --- Core packages ---
\usepackage{tikz}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{amssymb}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{etoolbox}  % \ifblank for robust empty-arg checks (\WavyChain trap_csv)
\usepackage{xstring}   % \IfSubStr — used by L3 snippet macros (polymer_chain)
\usepackage{chemfig}   % optional chemistry labels/future molecular snippets
\usepackage{pgfplots}  % v0.3 L3 snippet library: function-evaluated axes/curves
\pgfplotsset{compat=1.18}

%% --- paper loglog style key (A2 log_plot snippet) ---
%% Apply in any pgfplots axis to enforce Style Lock + paper-grade tick density.
%% Caller specifies width/height as positional args; everything else inherits.
%% Caller still picks xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax/xtick/ytick + plot data and uses
%% \PlotCallout for plot labels that need a leader instead of bare inline nodes.
%% Do NOT enable `log ticks with fixed point` — the default 10^k format is correct.
%% `scale only axis` is load-bearing: without it, PGFPlots W/H includes title +
%% xlabel + tick labels in the bbox, so a panel with title= ends up with a
%% smaller inner plot area than a sibling without title — breaking visual
%% parity across panels that share width/height. Surfaced 2026-05-04 from
%% golden Row 1 (Discharge title= squeezed plot area). Do not remove.
%% Spike-validated 2026-05-04 (G1-G3) on golden_trap_depth_picture.
\pgfplotsset{
  paper loglog/.style 2 args={
    width=#1, height=#2,
    scale only axis,
    axis line style={cGray, line width=0.38pt},
    tick style={cGray, line width=0.25pt},
    tick label style={font=\sffamily\fontsize{5.0}{6.0}\selectfont, text=cGray!90!black},
    label style={font=\sffamily\fontsize{5.5}{6.6}\selectfont, text=cGray},
    title style={font=\sffamily\itshape\fontsize{6.5}{7.8}\selectfont, text=black, yshift=-2pt},
    grid style={cGray!30, dashed, line width=0.15pt},
    minor grid style={cGray!25, dashed, line width=0.12pt},
    xminorgrids=true, yminorgrids=true,
    log basis x=10, log basis y=10,
    every axis plot/.append style={line width=0.92pt},
  }
}

%% --- chemfig defaults for optional chemistry-aware schematics ---
%% Caller may override via local \setchemfig{...} in figure scope.
\setchemfig{
  atom sep=0.30cm,
  bond offset=0.5pt,
  double bond sep=1.2pt,
  bond style={draw=cGray, line width=0.85pt},
}
%% Atom labels: sans-serif Arial; sulfur amber, others palette gray.
%% Detection is by exact-string compare on the atom token (chemfig passes raw atom name).
\makeatletter
\renewcommand*\printatom[1]{%
  \sffamily\fontsize{6.0}{7.2}\selectfont
  \def\fa@tempa{#1}\def\fa@tempS{S}%
  \ifx\fa@tempa\fa@tempS
    \textcolor{cAmber}{#1}%
  \else
    \textcolor{cGray}{#1}%
  \fi
}
\makeatother

%% --- TikZ libraries ---
\usetikzlibrary{
  arrows.meta,
  calc,
  backgrounds,
  shapes.geometric,
  shadings,
  positioning
}

%% --- Paul Tol Muted palette (sc7 v6/v7 standard) ---
\definecolor{cAmber}{HTML}{997A1E}
\definecolor{cBlue}{HTML}{4477AA}
\definecolor{cRed}{HTML}{CC6677}
\definecolor{cTeal}{HTML}{44AA99}
\definecolor{cGray}{RGB}{70,70,70}
\definecolor{cLGray}{RGB}{200,200,200}
\definecolor{cBrown}{HTML}{5D4820}
\definecolor{cArmAmber}{HTML}{8B6F3E}
\definecolor{cAmberSphere}{HTML}{DDCC77}

%% --- 2D trap-level schematic primitives ---
%% \TrapLevel{x}{y}{line-style}: horizontal trap state plus electron marker.
%% The third argument accepts a TikZ style or color expression.
\newcommand{\TrapLevel}[3]{%
  \draw[#3] (#1-0.18,#2) -- (#1+0.22,#2);
  \node[
    circle,
    draw=cBlue!75!black,
    fill=cBlue!45,
    inner sep=0pt,
    minimum size=1.35mm,
    line width=0.34pt
  ] at (#1,#2+0.13) {};
}

%% --- Fixed-view isometric schematic contract ---
%% Projection basis:
%%   x axis = (1.00cm, 0.00cm)
%%   y axis = (0.45cm, 0.28cm)
%%   z axis = (0.00cm, 1.00cm)
%% Use for schematic depth cues only. This is not a general 3D camera.
%% Note: \IsoBlock defines named coordinates isoA-isoH inside \begingroup..\endgroup,
%% but TikZ named coordinates are global. Sequential \IsoBlock calls overwrite them
%% (fine). Do not nest \IsoBlock calls inside another's 5th-arg hook -- silent
%% coordinate collisions will result.
\tikzset{
  iso basis/.style={
    x={(1.00cm,0.00cm)},
    y={(0.45cm,0.28cm)},
    z={(0.00cm,1.00cm)}
  },
  iso front face/.style={draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=#1!28},
  iso top face/.style={draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=#1!18},
  iso side face/.style={draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=#1!38},
  iso edge/.style={draw=cGray!85!black, line width=0.55pt},
  iso hidden edge/.style={draw=cGray!45, line width=0.35pt, dashed},
  iso label/.style={font=\fontsize{7}{8.4}\selectfont, text=cGray!90!black},
  iso callout/.style={draw=cGray!70, line width=0.35pt, -{Stealth[length=3pt,width=2pt]}},
  iso charge/.style={draw=cBlue!80!black, fill=cBlue!70, line width=0.25pt},
  iso exposed/.style={draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=#1!12},
}

% \IsoBlock{w}{d}{h}{color}{hook}: isometric block (W x D x H) at origin; #5 is TikZ code executed inside the iso group.
\newcommand{\IsoBlock}[5]{%
  \begingroup
  \def\isow{#1}%
  \def\isod{#2}%
  \def\isoh{#3}%
  \def\isocolor{#4}%
  \coordinate (isoA) at (0,0,0);
  \coordinate (isoB) at (\isow,0,0);
  \coordinate (isoC) at (\isow,\isod,0);
  \coordinate (isoD) at (0,\isod,0);
  \coordinate (isoE) at (0,0,\isoh);
  \coordinate (isoF) at (\isow,0,\isoh);
  \coordinate (isoG) at (\isow,\isod,\isoh);
  \coordinate (isoH) at (0,\isod,\isoh);
  \path[iso side face=\isocolor] (isoB) -- (isoC) -- (isoG) -- (isoF) -- cycle;
  \path[iso front face=\isocolor] (isoA) -- (isoB) -- (isoF) -- (isoE) -- cycle;
  \path[iso top face=\isocolor] (isoE) -- (isoF) -- (isoG) -- (isoH) -- cycle;
  \draw[iso edge] (isoA) -- (isoB) -- (isoC) -- (isoG) -- (isoH) -- (isoE) -- cycle;
  \draw[iso edge] (isoB) -- (isoF) -- (isoG);
  \draw[iso edge] (isoE) -- (isoF);
  \draw[iso hidden edge] (isoA) -- (isoD) -- (isoC);
  \draw[iso hidden edge] (isoD) -- (isoH);
  #5%
  \endgroup
}

% \IsoCharge{x,y,sign}: half-buried hemisphere charge dot (★★★ v0.2 promotion)
% Spec §8.
\newcommand{\IsoCharge}[1]{%
  \IsoChargeDraw#1\relax
}
\def\IsoChargeDraw#1,#2,#3\relax{%
  \begin{scope}
    \pgfmathsetmacro{\ICRADIUS}{0.12}%
    \fill[iso charge] (#1,#2) +(-\ICRADIUS,0)
      arc[start angle=180, end angle=360, radius=\ICRADIUS] -- cycle;
    \node[font=\tiny\bfseries, white] at (#1,#2) {$#3$};
  \end{scope}%
}

% \GradSlab{x,y,w,h,c1,c2,axis}: rectangular slab with x/y color gradient.
\newcommand{\GradSlab}[1]{%
  \GradSlabDraw#1\relax
}
\makeatletter
\def\GradSlabDraw#1,#2,#3,#4,#5,#6,#7\relax{%
  \begingroup
  \edef\GSAxis{\zap@space#7 \@empty}%
  \def\GSXAxis{x}%
  \ifx\GSAxis\GSXAxis
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, shade,
      left color=#5!35, right color=#6!35] (#1,#2) rectangle ++(#3,#4);
  \else
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, shade,
      bottom color=#5!35, top color=#6!35] (#1,#2) rectangle ++(#3,#4);
  \fi
  \endgroup
}
\makeatother

% \IsoConeTip{x,y,direction}: triangular tip emphasis face.
\newcommand{\IsoConeTip}[1]{%
  \IsoConeTipDraw#1\relax
}
\makeatletter
\def\IsoConeTipDraw#1,#2,#3\relax{%
  \begingroup
  \edef\ICTDir{\zap@space#3 \@empty}%
  \def\ICTRight{right}%
  \def\ICTLeft{left}%
  \def\ICTUp{up}%
  \def\ICTDown{down}%
  \ifx\ICTDir\ICTRight
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=cAmberSphere!75]
      (#1,#2) -- ++(-0.30,0.16) -- ++(0,-0.32) -- cycle;
  \else\ifx\ICTDir\ICTLeft
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=cAmberSphere!75]
      (#1,#2) -- ++(0.30,0.16) -- ++(0,-0.32) -- cycle;
  \else\ifx\ICTDir\ICTUp
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=cAmberSphere!75]
      (#1,#2) -- ++(-0.16,-0.30) -- ++(0.32,0) -- cycle;
  \else\ifx\ICTDir\ICTDown
    \path[draw=cGray!80!black, line width=0.45pt, fill=cAmberSphere!75]
      (#1,#2) -- ++(-0.16,0.30) -- ++(0.32,0) -- cycle;
  \else
    \PackageError{polymer-paper-preamble}{Invalid IsoConeTip direction `#3'}{Use right, left, up, or down.}%
  \fi\fi\fi\fi
  \endgroup
}
\makeatother

% \BandBox{x}{y}{label}: filled cLGray rectangle node used as CB or VB block.
\providecommand{\BandBox}[3]{%

% \PlotCallout[<node style>]{target coord}{label coord}{text}: white-backed plot annotation
% with a short leader arrow. Coordinates may be plain TikZ or pgfplots axis coords.
\newcommand{\PlotCallout}[4][]{%
  \draw[plotCalloutLine] #3 -- #2;
  \node[plotCalloutLabel, #1] at #3 {#4};
}

% \BellCurve[<style keys>]{x1,y1,x2,y2,orientation}: symmetric bell-lobe shape primitive.
% Geometry: bbox (x1,y1)-(x2,y2); orientation in {up, side}.
% Style: caller supplies draw/fill/line width via the optional [#1] pgfkeys list,
% or via \tikzset{bell curve/.append style={...}} for figure-wide consistency.
% Default `bell curve/.style` is outline-only with palette-neutral cGray; the `fill`
% key is intentionally omitted (TikZ semantic — see design spec §"Empirically validated").
\tikzset{
  bell curve/.style={
    draw=cGray,
    line width=0.5pt,
  },
}
\makeatletter
\newcommand{\BellCurve}[2][]{%
  \BellCurve@parse[#1]#2\relax
}
\def\BellCurve@parse[#1]#2,#3,#4,#5,#6\relax{%
  \pgfmathsetmacro{\BC@w}{(#4)-(#2)}%
  \pgfmathsetmacro{\BC@h}{(#5)-(#3)}%
  \edef\BC@orient{\zap@space#6 \@empty}%
  \def\BC@up{up}\def\BC@side{side}%
  \ifx\BC@orient\BC@up
    \path[bell curve, #1]
      (#2,#3)
      .. controls (#2+0.207*\BC@w,#3+0.043*\BC@h)
              and (#2+0.264*\BC@w,#3+0.986*\BC@h)
      .. (#2+0.494*\BC@w,#5)
      .. controls (#2+0.724*\BC@w,#5)
              and (#2+0.747*\BC@w,#3+0.058*\BC@h)
      .. (#4,#3) -- cycle;
  \else\ifx\BC@orient\BC@side
    \path[bell curve, #1]
      (#2,#3)
      .. controls (#2+0.043*\BC@w,#3+0.207*\BC@h)
              and (#2+0.986*\BC@w,#3+0.264*\BC@h)
      .. (#4,#3+0.494*\BC@h)
      .. controls (#4,#3+0.724*\BC@h)
              and (#2+0.058*\BC@w,#3+0.747*\BC@h)
      .. (#2,#5) -- cycle;
  \else
    \PackageError{polymer-paper-preamble}{Invalid BellCurve orientation `#6'}{Use up or side.}%
  \fi\fi
}
\makeatother
